title: Seafloor device and method of using a seafloor device
abstract: A device, method and system are described for performing soil testing. The device comprises a cone rod unit (110), a control unit (150) and at least one intermediate rod unit (120) arranged between the cone rod unit (110) and the control unit (150). The cone rod unit (110) comprises a power source and 5 at least one light source. The control unit (150) comprises a light sensor arranged to detect light emitted by the at least one light source of the cone rod unit (110). The at least one intermediate rod unit (120) is arranged to guide the light emitted by the at least one light source of the cone rod unit (110) to the light sensor of the control unit (150).
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A device for performing a test to determine soil properties from a seafloor, the device comprising: a cone rod unit (1 10) comprising: o a cone rod interface unit (210) comprising at least one first light source (212) configured to generate a first light signal containing first data; a control unit (150) comprising: o a first sensor (250) configured to detect said first light signal containing said first data; and o a communication unit (152) configured to transmit and receive data to and from a data acquisition unit (130); and one or more intermediate rod units (120) arranged between the cone rod unit (1 10) and the control unit (150), configured to guide said first light signal from the cone rod interface unit (210) to the first sensor (250) of the control unit (150). 2. The device of claim 1 , wherein the first light signal comprises a modulated signal where the modulation is at least one of time modulation, frequency modulation, phase modulation and amplitude modulation. 3. The device of claim 1 or 2, wherein device is configured with at least one of the following features: the cone rod unit (1 10) further comprises a first light guiding element; and each intermediate rod unit (120) comprises a respective intermediate light guiding element (620). 4. The device of claim 3, wherein at least one of the first light guiding element and each intermediate light guiding element (620) are made of at least one of a glass and a polymer. 5. The device of claim 3 or claim 4, wherein at least one of the first light guiding element (610) and each intermediate light guiding element (620) is internally coated with a light reflecting layer. 6. The device of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one first light source (212) comprises at least one of a light emitting diode, LED and at least one laser. 7. The device of claim 6, wherein the at least one of a LED and the at least one laser emits at least one of blue, violet and green light. 8. The device of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cone rod unit (1 10) further comprises a lens (510) arranged in a light path of the at least one first light source (212). 9. The device of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the control unit (150) further comprises a second light source (312) configured to generate a second light signal containing second data, and the cone rod interface unit (210) further comprises a second sensor (214) configured to detect said second light signal emitted by the second light source (312). 10. The device of claim 3, wherein the first light guiding element and a first intermediate light guiding element of a first intermediate rod unit of the at least one intermediate rod unit are arranged to be separated by a light coupling gap. 1 1 . The device of claim 3, wherein intermediate light guiding elements of adjacent intermediate rod units of the at least one intermediate rod unit are arranged to be separated by a light coupling gap. 12. The device of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: at least one repeater intermediate rod unit (120â), comprising: a first intermediate light source (614) disposed in a first end of the at least one repeater intermediate rod unit (120â); an intermediate power source (616) disposed in the at least one repeater intermediate rod unit (120â); a first intermediate light sensor (612) disposed in a second end of the at least one repeater intermediate rod unit (120â), opposite the first end of the at least one repeater intermediate rod unit (120â). 13. A system for automatically performing a test to determine soil properties comprising: a cone rod unit (1 10) comprising at least one sensor (142) and at least one light source (212), the at least one light source (212) being configured to generate a first light signal containing first data; a control unit (150) configured to be arranged at a position above a test position on a seafloor at which the test is to be performed, the position and the test position defining a firing line (709) therebetween; a frame (707) configured to: o collect a first intermediate rod unit (120) from one or more intermediate rod units from a rod storage region (71 1), o position the first intermediate rod unit in the firing line, directly above the cone rod unit (1 10), such that the first intermediate rod unit (120) is axially aligned with the cone rod unit (1 10), o attach the first intermediate rod unit (120) to the cone rod unit (1 10) to assemble a test string, o push the assembled test string into soil below the seafloor; and o hold the test string during assembly; and 

 wherein the cone rod unit (1 10) is configured to transmit said first light signal to the control unit (150) when the frame (707) pushes the assembled test string into said soil, and the control unit (150) is configured to sense the transmitted first light signal and communicate with a remote data acquisition unit. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the frame (707) is further configured to collect a second intermediate rod unit (120) from the plurality of intermediate rod units from the rod storage region (71 1), position the second intermediate rod unit in the firing line (709), directly above the first intermediate rod unit, such that the second intermediate rod unit is axially aligned with the first intermediate rod unit, and attach the second intermediate rod unit to the first intermediate rod unit to extend the test string. 15. The system of claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the frame (707) is configured to push the assembled test string into the soil at a constant speed of between 1 -5 cm/s, for instance, approximately 2 cm/s. 16. A method of performing a test to determine soil properties using the system of any one of claims 13 to 15, the method comprising: holding the cone rod unit (1 10) in the firing line (709); attaching the first intermediate rod unit (120) of the one or more of intermediate rod units to the cone rod unit (1 10) to assemble a test string, the first intermediate rod unit and the cone rod unit being axially aligned; pushing the assembled test string into the soil of the seafloor; transmitting a light signal from the at least one light source of the cone rod unit to the light sensor of the control unit, and transmitting a signal from the control unit to the data acquisition unit; holding the assembled test string in the firing line; and extending the test string by attaching at least a second intermediate rod unit of the plurality of intermediate rod units to the assembled test string. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the operation of extending the test string is repeated until the test string is between 20 m and 100 m in length, preferably between 50 and 80 m in length. 18. The method of claim 16 or claim 17, wherein the operation of pushing the assembled test string into the soil of the seafloor comprises pushing the assembled test string into the soil of the seafloor at a constant speed of between 1 -5 m/s, for instance, approximately 2 cm/s.
